markdownRemark.html:
While tidying my bookmarks today, I found a load of sites I’d used for my MSc. I thought I might as well post them here before I delete them as it took me a while to collect them all and they may be useful for somebody somewhere.

  • How many internet and PC users are there in the world?{#rdf:#$vPMLd1}
  • Design will save the world{#rdf:#$sPMLd1}
  • Optimal Web Design (Psychology){#rdf:#$tPMLd1}
  • A framework for effective commercial Web application development{#rdf:#$wPMLd1}
  • World Language Statistics{#rdf:#$DPMLd1}
  • Omniglot - a guide to abjads, alphabets, syllabaries and other writing systems{#rdf:#$EPMLd1}
  • Microsoft pays dear for insults through ignorance
  • Global digital divide ‘narrowing’{#rdf:#$HPMLd1}
  • Application Development in the ‘Global Village’{#rdf:#$IPMLd1}
  • The “anyone can be provincial!” page{#rdf:#$KPMLd1}
  • Microsoft pays dear for insults through ignorance{#rdf:#$LPMLd1}
  • Problems with the current Localization Framework in ASP.NET and what’s coming in ASP.NET 2.0{#rdf:#$MPMLd1}
  • Punycode - Wikipedia{#rdf:#$NPMLd1}
  • Global By Design: Taking The Web Worldwide{#rdf:#$OPMLd1}
  • Localisation of UI with .Net{#rdf:#$PPMLd1}
  • What not-for-profit organizations need to know about free software{#rdf:#$VPMLd1}
  • CompassPoint | ASP Guide (PDF){#rdf:#$WPMLd1}
  • ebase® - Community Relationship Management for Nonprofits{#rdf:#$XPMLd1}
  • Specialist Software for Voluntary Sector{#rdf:#$YPMLd1}
  • SocialSoftware.org{#rdf:#$ZPMLd1}
  • Creating multilingual websites{#rdf:#$QPMLd1}
  • The revenge of geography{#rdf:#$RPMLd1}
  • ISO 646 (Good old ASCII){#rdf:#$SPMLd1}
  • Website globalization resources: articles and links{#rdf:#$TPMLd1}
  • IT 4 Communities - Case Studies{#rdf:#$1QMLd1}
  • Vivid Orange{#rdf:#$2QMLd1}
  • Office of the e-Envoy{#rdf:#$3QMLd1}
  • nfpSynergy{#rdf:#$4QMLd1}
  • Journal article on Civic and Community Technology{#rdf:#$5QMLd1}
